results.court: Supreme Court of the United States

opinions.snippet: The right to marry is a fundamental right inherent in the liberty of the person, and under the Due Process and Equal Protection Clauses of the Fourteenth Amendment couples of the same-sex may not be deprived of that right and that liberty.

results.court: Supreme Court of the United States

opinions.snippet: Qualified immunity protects government officials from liability for civil damages insofar as their conduct does not violate clearly established statutory or constitutional rights of which a reasonable person would have known. The Court confirmed the two-step sequence is often appropriate but not mandatory.
